part_title: Part One: Introduction, Foundations, and General Principles of Integrated Pest Management
part_short_title: Introduction, Foundations, and General Principles of Integrated Pest Management
part_subtitle: Integrated Pest Management of Tuta absoluta

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

Tuta absoluta is a pest that is difficult to manage using a single control method because of its biological and behavioral characteristics and its high capacity for reproduction, dispersal, and concealment within plant tissues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is a pest that is difficult to manage using a single control method because of its biological and behavioral characteristics and its high capacity for reproduction, dispersal, and concealment within plant tissues.

items.text: The pest possesses several characteristics that increase the difficulty of control, most importantly:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The pest possesses several characteristics that increase the difficulty of control, most importantly:

items: High fecundity, which may reach approximately 260 eggs per female and may approach 300 eggs under favorable conditions.
Short generation time under warm conditions.
Production of numerous overlapping generations during the year.
Simultaneous presence of eggs, larvae, pupae, and adult moths in the field.
Larval feeding within mines in leaves, stems, and fruits.
Difficulty of surface-applied insecticides reaching larvae after they enter plant tissues.
Ability of advanced larvae to leave mines and move to other plant organs or plants.
Pupation of a proportion of larvae in soil or plant residues.
Persistence of the pest on volunteer tomato plants, black nightshade, and other solanaceous hosts.
Movement of moths from old and neglected fields into newly planted crops.
Rapid development of resistance when insecticides with the same mode of action are repeatedly used.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]High fecundity, which may reach approximately 260 eggs per female and may approach 300 eggs under favorable conditions.
[[prepared]]Production of numerous overlapping generations during the year.
[[prepared]]Simultaneous presence of eggs, larvae, [[term:pupation]]pupae[[/term]], and adult moths in the field.
[[prepared]]Larval feeding within [[term:mines]]mines[[/term]] in leaves, stems, and fruits.
[[prepared]]Difficulty of surface-applied insecticides reaching larvae after they enter plant tissues.
[[prepared]]Ability of advanced larvae to leave mines and move to other plant organs or plants.
[[prepared]]Pupation of a proportion of larvae in soil or plant residues.
[[prepared]]Persistence of the pest on volunteer tomato plants, black nightshade, and other [[term:solanaceae]]solanaceous hosts[[/term]].
[[prepared]]Movement of moths from old and neglected fields into newly planted crops.
[[prepared]]Rapid development of resistance when insecticides with the same [[term:mode-of-action]]mode of action[[/term]] are repeatedly used.

items.text: These characteristics make complete eradication of Tuta absoluta within an open agricultural area an unrealistic objective in most situations. Integrated pest management therefore does not aim to eliminate every individual pest, but to keep pest populations and resulting damage below the level that causes unacceptable economic loss.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These characteristics make [[term:eradication]]complete eradication[[/term]] of Tuta absoluta within an open agricultural area an unrealistic objective in most situations. [[term:ipm]]Integrated pest management[[/term]] therefore does not aim to eliminate every individual pest, but to keep pest populations and resulting damage below the level that causes unacceptable economic loss.

items.text: Sustainable management is achieved by preventing the pest from reaching high densities rather than waiting for a severe infestation to develop and then attempting to reduce it through a succession of sprays.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sustainable management is achieved by preventing the pest from reaching high densities rather than waiting for a severe infestation to develop and then attempting to reduce it through a succession of sprays.

items.text: An integrated management program begins before planting, continues through all crop stages, and ends after harvest with the removal of pest carryover sources.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An integrated management program begins before planting, continues through all crop stages, and ends after harvest with the removal of pest carryover sources.

items.text: Why Is Chemical Control Alone Insufficient?
Insecticides may rapidly reduce some stages of Tuta absoluta, but they do not reach all individuals in the pest population equally.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Insecticides may rapidly reduce some stages of Tuta absoluta, but they do not reach all individuals in the pest population equally.

items.text: Eggs may be less exposed to some products, larvae inside mines or fruits may be partially protected, pupae in soil or plant residues are not reached by foliar sprays, and moths may continue immigrating from neighboring fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs may be less exposed to some products, larvae inside mines or fruits may be partially protected, pupae in soil or plant residues are not reached by foliar sprays, and moths may continue immigrating from neighboring fields.

items.text: Continuous dependence on insecticides alone leads to problems including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Continuous dependence on insecticides alone leads to problems including:

items: Reinfestation after residual insecticidal activity declines.
Declining efficacy of products over time.
Contamination of soil, irrigation water, and the surrounding environment.
Increased risk to workers and consumers.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Contamination of soil, irrigation water, and the surrounding environment.

items.text: The presence of old mines on leaves may also lead to unnecessary spraying even though the larvae have already left or died.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The presence of old mines on leaves may also lead to unnecessary spraying even though the larvae have already left or died.

items.text: Chemical control is therefore one tool within an integrated pest-management program and not the entire program.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Chemical control is therefore one tool within an integrated pest-management program and not the entire program.

items.text: Definition of Integrated Pest Management

Integrated pest management is a scientific decision-making system based on the study of the pest, crop, and agricultural environment. It combines preventive, monitoring, cultural, mechanical, behavioral, biological, and chemical methods to reduce pest populations to an economically and environmentally acceptable level.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management is a scientific decision-making system based on the study of the pest, crop, and agricultural environment. It combines preventive, monitoring, cultural, mechanical, behavioral, biological, and chemical methods to reduce pest populations to an economically and environmentally acceptable level.

items.text: The word:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The word:

items.text: Integrated
Means that different methods are not used separately or randomly, but are coordinated so that they complement one another.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Means that different methods are not used separately or randomly, but are coordinated so that they complement one another.

items.text: The word:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The word:

items.text: Pest
Means that the decision focuses on the organism causing actual economic damage rather than merely on the presence of an insect in the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Means that the decision focuses on the organism causing actual economic damage rather than merely on the presence of an insect in the field.

items.text: The word:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The word:

items.text: Management
Means regulating pest populations and limiting damage rather than necessarily achieving complete eradication.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Means regulating pest populations and limiting damage rather than necessarily achieving complete eradication.

items.text: Integrated management of Tuta absoluta can therefore be defined as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated management of Tuta absoluta can therefore be defined as:

items.text: > A dynamic system based on prevention, early detection, and quantitative assessment of infestation, followed by integration of cultural, mechanical, behavioral, biological, and selective chemical methods to keep the pest below the economic injury level while conserving natural enemies and reducing resistance, residues, and environmental risks.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]> A dynamic system based on prevention, early detection, and quantitative assessment of infestation, followed by integration of cultural, mechanical, behavioral, biological, and selective chemical methods to keep the pest below the economic injury level while conserving natural enemies and reducing resistance, residues, and environmental risks.

items.text: What Integrated Pest Management Does Not Mean
Integrated pest management does not mean:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management does not mean:

items: Merely replacing a chemical insecticide with a biological product.
Installing a pheromone trap and then neglecting plant inspection.
Mixing several different insecticides in one tank.
Applying a fixed set of treatments in every field.
Reducing all insects present in the field.
Removing all weeds without understanding their role.
Introducing natural enemies only after infestation reaches outbreak level.
Considering every leaf mine an active infestation.
Depending solely on trap catches for decision-making.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Merely replacing a chemical insecticide with a biological product.
[[prepared]]Installing a [[term:pheromone-trap]]pheromone trap[[/term]] and then neglecting plant inspection.
[[prepared]]Mixing several different insecticides in one tank.
[[prepared]]Applying a fixed set of treatments in every field.
[[prepared]]Reducing all insects present in the field.
[[prepared]]Removing all weeds without understanding their role.
[[prepared]]Introducing natural enemies only after infestation reaches outbreak level.
[[prepared]]Depending solely on trap catches for decision-making.

items.text: Integrated pest management is not a product that can be purchased. It is a decision-making system that changes according to the field, season, crop stage, and level of risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management is not a product that can be purchased. It is a decision-making system that changes according to the field, season, crop stage, and level of risk.

items.text: General Objective of Integrated Pest Management
The principal objective is to produce a sound and economically viable crop while keeping Tuta absoluta populations below the economic injury level and minimizing adverse effects on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The principal objective is to produce a sound and economically viable crop while keeping Tuta absoluta populations below the economic injury level and minimizing adverse effects on:

items.text: Program success is not measured only by the number of insects killed, but by the final economic and environmental outcome.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Program success is not measured only by the number of insects killed, but by the final economic and environmental outcome.

items.text: Detailed Objectives of Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Integrated management aims to achieve the following:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated management aims to achieve the following:

items: 1. Prevent pest entry into the nursery or field as far as possible.
3. Detect the pest while population density is still low.
4. Prevent establishment of an early generation within the crop.
6. Target young larvae before they penetrate deeply into plant tissues.
7. Prevent larval movement from leaves into growing points, stems, and fruits.
8. Reduce the percentage of infested fruits.
9. Prevent pest persistence in crop residues.
10. Break the green bridge between planting seasons.
12. Enhance the efficacy of biological control.
15. Comply with preharvest intervals and maximum residue limits.
16. Reduce the cost of producing a sound metric ton of crop.
17. Increase the percentage of marketable or processable yield.
18. Prevent movement of infestation into neighboring fields.
19. Document infestation history and intervention results.
20. Develop a local program suited to the region, planting season, and production system.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Prevent pest entry into the nursery or field as far as possible.
[[prepared]]3. Detect the pest while population density is still low.
[[prepared]]4. Prevent establishment of an early generation within the crop.
[[prepared]]6. Target young larvae before they penetrate deeply into plant tissues.
[[prepared]]7. Prevent larval movement from leaves into growing points, stems, and fruits.
[[prepared]]8. Reduce the percentage of infested fruits.
[[prepared]]9. Prevent pest persistence in crop residues.
[[prepared]]10. Break the [[term:green-bridge]]green bridge[[/term]] between planting seasons.
[[prepared]]12. Enhance the efficacy of biological control.
[[prepared]]15. Comply with preharvest intervals and [[term:pesticide-safety]]maximum residue limits[[/term]].
[[prepared]]16. Reduce the cost of producing a sound metric ton of crop.
[[prepared]]17. Increase the percentage of marketable or processable yield.
[[prepared]]18. Prevent movement of infestation into neighboring fields.
[[prepared]]19. Document infestation history and intervention results.
[[prepared]]20. Develop a local program suited to the region, planting season, and production system.

items.text: Scientific Foundations of Integrated Pest Management
Integrated pest management is based on a set of interrelated scientific foundations.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management is based on a set of interrelated scientific foundations.

items.text: Biological Foundation
The program depends on knowledge of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program depends on knowledge of:

items.text: This knowledge determines:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This knowledge determines:

items: The stage that should be targeted.
The expected period before appearance of the next generation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The expected period before appearance of the next generation.

items.text: Targeting a young larva before it enters a fruit is more effective than attempting to kill it after it has become established inside the fruit.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Targeting a young larva before it enters a fruit is more effective than attempting to kill it after it has become established inside the fruit.

items.text: Ecological Foundation
Tuta absoluta does not live separately from the agricultural environment. It interacts with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta does not live separately from the agricultural environment. It interacts with:

items.text: A decision should therefore not be based on pest number alone, but on the condition of the entire agroecosystem.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A decision should therefore not be based on pest number alone, but on the condition of the entire agroecosystem.

items.text: The presence of a particular number of eggs accompanied by high activity of egg parasitoids may require a different decision than the same egg density in the absence of natural enemies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The presence of a particular number of eggs accompanied by high activity of egg parasitoids may require a different decision than the same egg density in the absence of natural enemies.

items.text: Economic Foundation
Not every occurrence of the pest justifies implementation of a control treatment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not every occurrence of the pest justifies implementation of a control treatment.

items.text: The decision compares:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The decision compares:

items: Requirements of the market or processing plant.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Requirements of the market or processing plant.

items.text: The program aims to prevent the pest from reaching the economic injury level.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program aims to prevent the pest from reaching the economic injury level.

items.text: Monitoring and Information Foundation
Integrated pest management cannot exist without regular field information.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management cannot exist without regular field information.

items.text: Information is obtained from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Information is obtained from:

items: Inspection of old and neighboring fields.

items.text: Data should be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Data should be:

items.text: Preventive Foundation
Prevention is more effective and less costly than treating a severe outbreak.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Prevention is more effective and less costly than treating a severe outbreak.

items.text: Preventive measures include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preventive measures include:

items: Avoiding retention of old fields after harvesting has stopped.
Protecting greenhouses with suitable insect-proof screens.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoiding retention of old fields after harvesting has stopped.

items.text: Integration of Control Methods
Integrated management does not depend merely on accumulating numerous methods, but on selecting compatible methods.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated management does not depend merely on accumulating numerous methods, but on selecting compatible methods.

items.text: A program may fail when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A program may fail when:

items: An insecticide kills the released parasitoid.
Plants supporting natural enemies are removed without justification.
Pheromone traps are used without managing old fields.
Natural enemies are released only after infestation has become extremely high.
A single insecticide is repeatedly used despite declining efficacy.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Plants supporting natural enemies are removed without justification.
[[prepared]]Pheromone traps are used without managing old fields.
[[prepared]]Natural enemies are released only after infestation has become extremely high.
[[prepared]]A single insecticide is repeatedly used despite declining efficacy.

items.text: True integration means that each method performs a specific function without cancelling the effect of other methods.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]True integration means that each method performs a specific function without cancelling the effect of other methods.

items.text: Priority for Lower-Risk Methods
Priority is given to methods that provide acceptable pest reduction with the least possible harm, including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Priority is given to methods that provide acceptable pest reduction with the least possible harm, including:

items.text: Chemical insecticides are used when necessary, not merely because the pest is present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Chemical insecticides are used when necessary, not merely because the pest is present.

items.text: Local Management Foundation
No single fixed program is suitable for all regions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single fixed program is suitable for all regions.

items.text: The program changes according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program changes according to:

items.text: Recommendations should therefore be based on actual field conditions rather than direct copying of a program from another region.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Recommendations should therefore be based on actual field conditions rather than direct copying of a program from another region.

items.text: Continuous Evaluation
A treatment does not end when it is applied.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A treatment does not end when it is applied.

items.text: The field must be inspected afterward to determine:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field must be inspected afterward to determine:

items: Continued infestation of growing points or fruits.
Effect of the treatment on natural enemies.
Possibility of resistance or poor spray coverage.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Continued infestation of growing points or fruits.
[[prepared]]Effect of the treatment on natural enemies.
[[prepared]]Possibility of resistance or poor spray coverage.

items.text: Evaluation is used to modify subsequent management decisions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Evaluation is used to modify subsequent management decisions.

items.text: General Principles of Integrated Pest Management
Integrated pest management is based on eight sequential general principles.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management is based on eight sequential general principles.

items.text: Principle One: Prevention and Suppression of Pest Increase
Management begins before damage appears.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management begins before damage appears.

items.text: Preventive measures include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preventive measures include:

items: Prevention of pest movement among fields.

items.text: The objective is to make the agricultural environment less favorable for pest establishment and multiplication.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The objective is to make the agricultural environment less favorable for [[term:establishment]]pest establishment[[/term]] and multiplication.

items.text: Principle Two: Diagnosis and Regular Monitoring
The identity of the pest must be confirmed and distinguished from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The identity of the pest must be confirmed and distinguished from:

items.text: The pest is then monitored with suitable tools including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The pest is then monitored with suitable tools including:

items.text: Decisions should not be based on a single observation or general impression.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Decisions should not be based on a single observation or general impression.

items.text: Principle Three: Threshold-Based Decision-Making
Control methods should not be used merely because the pest is present.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Control methods should not be used merely because the pest is present.

items.text: The decision depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The decision depends on:

items.text: Local thresholds should be used when available. A threshold from another country or production system should not be transferred without evaluation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Local thresholds should be used when available. A threshold from another country or production system should not be transferred without evaluation.

items.text: Principle Four: Preference for Non-Chemical Methods
Cultural, mechanical, behavioral, and biological methods are given priority when they provide sufficient control.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Cultural, mechanical, behavioral, and biological methods are given priority when they provide sufficient control.

items.text: These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items.text: This does not mean rejecting chemical insecticides, but using them when lower-risk methods are insufficient.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This does not mean rejecting chemical insecticides, but using them when lower-risk methods are insufficient.

items.text: Principle Five: Selection of the Most Selective Method
When insecticides are used, the selected product should:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When insecticides are used, the selected product should:

items: Be registered for the crop and pest.
Cause less harm to natural enemies.
Pose minimal risk to pollinators and non-target organisms.
Be compatible with other program components.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Be registered for the crop and pest.
[[prepared]]Pose minimal risk to pollinators and non-target organisms.

items.text: Selection should be based not only on the trade name, but on the active ingredient and mode of action.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Selection should be based not only on the trade name, but on the [[term:active-ingredient]]active ingredient[[/term]] and mode of action.

items.text: Principle Six: Use of the Minimum Necessary Level of Intervention
Reducing pesticide use does not mean applying randomly reduced rates below the registered rate because an inadequate dose may fail and increase resistance risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reducing pesticide use does not mean applying randomly reduced rates below the registered rate because an inadequate dose may fail and increase resistance risk.

items.text: The minimum necessary level means:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The minimum necessary level means:

items: Avoiding sprays when they are unnecessary.
Preventing repeated treatment caused by poor application.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Preventing repeated treatment caused by poor application.

items.text: Use reduction must remain compatible with efficacy and must not increase resistance risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use reduction must remain compatible with efficacy and must not increase resistance risk.

items.text: Principle Seven: Insecticide Resistance Management
Tuta absoluta is a high-risk pest for resistance development because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is a high-risk pest for resistance development because of:

items: Repeated use of the same products.
Continuous selection pressure on successive generations.

items.text: Resistance management depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Resistance management depends on:

items: Avoiding treatment of successive generations with the same group.
Using effective products only when needed.
Complying with registered rate and timing.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoiding treatment of successive generations with the same group.

items.text: Changing the trade name does not mean changing the mode of action when the active ingredient or chemical group is the same.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Changing the trade name does not mean changing the mode of action when the active ingredient or chemical group is the same.

items.text: Principle Eight: Evaluation and Documentation of Results
Program success must be evaluated using quantitative indicators such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Program success must be evaluated using quantitative indicators such as:

items: Cost of producing one sound metric ton.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Cost of producing one sound metric ton.

items.text: All treatments and results should be recorded to allow:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]All treatments and results should be recorded to allow:

items.text: Specific Principles for Management of Tuta absoluta
In addition to the general principles, several principles are specific to the biology of Tuta absoluta.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In addition to the general principles, several principles are specific to the biology of Tuta absoluta.

items.text: Prevention of Early Establishment
The earlier the pest enters the crop, the more generations it can complete within the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The earlier the pest enters the crop, the more generations it can complete within the season.

items.text: Targeting Eggs and Young Larvae
Control is more effective before larvae enter deep mines and fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Control is more effective before larvae enter deep mines and fruits.

items.text: Prevention of Fruit Infestation
Fruit infestation is the point at which plant infestation becomes a direct economic loss.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fruit infestation is the point at which plant infestation becomes a direct economic loss.

items.text: Management of All Stages
The program should include methods targeting:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program should include methods targeting:

items.text: Management of Immigration
Control may succeed within the field and then infestation may recur because moths move from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Control may succeed within the field and then infestation may recur because moths move from:

items: A crop in which spraying has stopped.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A crop in which spraying has stopped.

items.text: Distinguishing Old Damage from Active Infestation
Treatments should not be based on dry old mines, but on eggs, live larvae, and new mines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatments should not be based on dry old mines, but on eggs, live larvae, and new mines.

items.text: Linking Traps with Plant Inspection
Traps alone are insufficient because they primarily monitor males and do not directly measure larval numbers or fruit infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps alone are insufficient because they primarily monitor males and do not directly measure larval numbers or fruit infestation.

items.text: Area-Wide Management
Management is more successful when neighboring farms cooperate in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management is more successful when neighboring farms cooperate in:

items.text: Hierarchy of Decision-Making
Integrated pest management follows this sequence:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated pest management follows this sequence:

items.text: Level One: Prevention
Prevent pest entry or reduce opportunities for establishment.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Prevent pest entry or reduce opportunities for establishment.

items.text: Level Two: Monitoring
Detect eggs, moths, and recent mines early.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Detect eggs, moths, and recent mines early.

items.text: Level Three: Assessment
Determine infestation density, dominant stage, and population trend.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Determine infestation density, dominant stage, and population trend.

items.text: Level Four: Decision
Determine whether intervention is required and select the appropriate intervention.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Determine whether intervention is required and select the appropriate intervention.

items.text: Level Five: Non-Chemical Methods
Apply cultural, behavioral, mechanical, and biological measures.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Apply cultural, behavioral, mechanical, and biological measures.

items.text: Level Six: Selective Chemical Intervention
Use when necessary, with correct selection of active ingredient, timing, and mode of action.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use when necessary, with correct selection of active ingredient, timing, and mode of action.

items.text: Level Seven: Evaluation
Measure the result after intervention.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure the result after intervention.

items.text: Level Eight: Modification
Modify the program according to monitoring and evaluation results.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Modify the program according to monitoring and evaluation results.

items.text: Indicators of Success of an Integrated Pest-Management Program
A program is considered successful when it achieves a set of outcomes, not merely temporary larval mortality.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A program is considered successful when it achieves a set of outcomes, not merely temporary larval mortality.

Reduction in numbers of live larvae.
Reduced movement of infestation from leaves into fruits.
Avoidance of repeated use of the same mode-of-action groups.
Reduced control cost per metric ton.
Prevention of the field becoming an infestation source for neighboring fields.

[[prepared]]Prevention of the field becoming an infestation source for neighboring fields.

items.text: Summary of the Foundational Part
Integrated management of Tuta absoluta is not a spray program, a single biological-control method, or a fixed set of treatments. It is an integrated decision-making system.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Integrated management of Tuta absoluta is not a spray program, a single biological-control method, or a fixed set of treatments. It is an integrated decision-making system.

items.text: It is based on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It is based on:

items: Use of selective products when necessary.

items.text: The program begins before the pest enters the field and continues until crop residues are removed after harvest.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The program begins before the pest enters the field and continues until crop residues are removed after harvest.

items.text: Preventing early establishment, targeting eggs and young larvae, conserving natural enemies, preventing fruit infestation, and managing immigration sources and resistance are the principal foundations of successful integrated management of Tuta absoluta.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preventing early establishment, targeting eggs and young larvae, conserving natural enemies, preventing fruit infestation, and managing immigration sources and resistance are the principal foundations of successful integrated management of Tuta absoluta.

meta.part_title: Part One: Introduction, Foundations, and General Principles of Integrated Pest Management
meta.part_subtitle: Integrated Pest Management of Tuta absoluta
